title: Continuous Ingestion, Reindexing, Cutover, and Disaster Recovery · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed retrieval review reconstructs continuous ingestion, reindexing, cutover, and disaster recovery from source, document, chunk, model, index, query, candidate, response, citation, evaluation, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then attacks one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for recoverable continuous-indexing system after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Capture creates, updates, moves, permissions, supersession, deletes, checkpoints, source snapshots, and out-of-order events from connectors.
Classify parse, policy, model, rate, storage, poison, and unknown failures with bounded retries, dead letters, replay identity, and operator evidence.
Build immutable candidate indexes, reconcile counts and versions, run shadow and quality checks, switch atomically, monitor, and retain rollback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review RAG engineering team handles case 7852 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to operate change capture, retries, dead letters, reindexing, blue-green cutover, deletion, and restore; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Vector indexes never need recovery planning because they can be regenerated.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose what to back up versus rebuild, retain source and configuration identity, test restore objectives, and verify retrieval and deletion after recovery. Artifact under review: recoverable continuous-indexing system.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient Rag Backup Restore Rebuild query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Rag Backup Restore Rebuild, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
